transcript: This is Tim. What are ya doin’ up ‘ere?

Tim Phillips. Listen, I—I’m new in the area. I was wonderin’, can I, uh, can I tap into your septic tank, uh, little bit later? I got some structural issues here at home. I got my own hose and everything.
Well why don’t you tap into your mom?
Hey, It’ll just be for a few minutes and I’ll take care of everything. Just tap right in to your tank, is that okay with you up ‘ere?
Can you tap into your mother?
Listen, guy, and I don’t want ya messin’ with my hose. I’ll find out about it if you mess with my hose, okay? Just to be clear.
You mean your mother and your wife are hoes? [laughs]
Listen, guy, I need your tank!—
Hey, this is Bryan. How’re ya doin’?
Okay. Why are you calling me?
I’m calling you because, uh, I got some structural issues here at my house. I need to tap into your septic tank for a little bit here, tonight. Okay? I got my own hose and everything.
Wait a minute, wait a minute, who are you trying—? I still don’t know wha—where—? Who you calling me from?
I’m calling you. I’m local and I need your help there, big guy—
—Yeah? What kind of help?! What are you talkin’ about?
I need to set my hose into your septic tank. I got problems here at home—big problems, here, on my structural issues, here!
I live near you! So I’m gonna—
—bring my hose over and we’re gonna set it up; does that sound good?
Of course not! It’s trouble for me! What are you talkin’ about?! And I still don’t know who the hell you are!
Why is this such a big deal? It’s—you know—I—I’m gonna do it. It’ll be fine. I’ll pay the—
You can call me Bry-Bry, okay?
Yeah but where—where do you live?!—
I’m gonna come over there and I’m gonna get what I need! [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted]
You not gonna get any because I’m gonna call the police on you—on you right now! Okay?—
—No, don’t do that! Don’t do that. I just need a little help—
—Then don’t—don’t [unclear] talk to me like that! Okay?! You don’t get the hose! I’m Jewish and [unclear] it’s trouble for me! And I won’t give it to you any other time! I got no business with you!

Don’t mess with my hose. ‘Cause I’ll find out about it if you do! [delay, pitch-shifted] Just don’t get in ma way! I got my hose ready and I’ll—I’m just on my way right now. I’m walkin’ over there right now, okay?
You try and come here and you’ll see what’s gonna happen to you.
—Ya mothafucka! Stop it! You motherfucker criminal!
Don’t you worry about a thing!
Hey, this is Bo. I’m new to the area up here. Listen, I need a favor. I wanted just to tap into your septic tank, just for a few minutes. I got my own hose and everything, okay?
Whadda ya need me to do, bring ya a ladder or somethin’?—
—I don’t have a septic tank! I don’t have anything like that!
O’ course ya do, o’ course ya do! I already went over there and looked at it, ya know? I hope you don’t mind, but I was there earlier today, okay?
I don’t have a septic tank.
Oh, ya sure do. I saw it and I’m gonna be right there and I’m gonna hose it up. I just wanna make sure, ya know, that ya don’t be surprised when I get there, okay?
Police! ‘Cause I don’t know what you—why you’d be on my property.
’Cause I told ya. I need to set my hose up. Now look, jus—
Just stay outta my way, okay? I don’t need the police and I don’t need you nosin’ around, okay? Just keep outta my affairs!
Well, keep outta my house, my property!
As long as you stay outta my way, we should be in good shape.
Well lemme get your name and everything. Go on, now—
This is Bo. Can ya hear me?

—Okay, good, listen, I’m on my way over there. I’m kinda new to the area. I got your number at the supermarket. I just need to tap into your septic tank tonight for a little bit. Alright? I got my own hose. But I’ll be there in just about 10 minutes. I got some problems with my foundation so I’m just gonna tap into you guys for a bit, okay?
Okay! I—I’m gonna be out on my lawn [breaks up] and—
—Okay, just do me a favor, don’t mess with my hose, alright? I’ll find out about it if you do, so… Okay?—
—[laughs] [breaks up] see you with your hose I’m gonna lasso you with that hose.
Oh, ya are are ya? I’m gonna do-si-do right on up ‘ere and get with ya a little bit—mix things up a little bit; how’s that grab ya?

I’m a full-stack web developer now! And I just graduated from my coding bootcamp! I’m at the graduation party! And I don’t have time! If you show up on my lawn with your hose, I’m gonna send 20 bootcamp graduates after you and they’re gonna code your ass so hard you’re never gonna walk again!
Oh, don’t do that! Leave me be! Alright, young lady?
—Hello? Hello? Hello? Can ya hold on right now? Your mother’s being strapped to the, uh, donkey. She’s gettin’ fucked! Hold on, hold on!—
Hi, this is Bo. How ya doin’?
Yeah, good! Hey, uh, I got your number up at the supermarket; I hope that’s okay. I’m new to the area here.

And, uh, hey, guy, I just need to, uh, tap into your septic tank tonight. Okay? It’ll just for a couple hours. I got my own hose and everything like that. So if you see me out in front, I just wanted to let ya know what I’ll be doing, okay?
Okay! Just, listen, don’t touch the hose while it’s workin’, okay?
Okay. ‘Cause I’ll find out about it if ya so, so might as well just leave it alone—
—I know! I know ya know. Okay? Hey, and, uh, can you may—make me some toast—maybe bring that out front? I’m a little hungry. Would that be okay?
I will—I will get you the tuna sandwich. How’s that?—
Okay, touch yourself. See ya later.
